review: Amazing week on the Red Sea, clear water, healthy reefs, and very professional dive guides.
Great diving and snorkeling, but the boat felt a bit crowded on one of the days.
Loved waking up early to hike the Sinai mountains, the sunrise at the summit was unforgettable.
Easy hikes, friendly Bedouin guides, and simple but tasty camp food.
The White Desert looks like another planet, camping under the stars here was the highlight of our trip.
Amazing scenery and a fun 4x4 ride, but I wish we had one more night in the desert.
So much history in Luxor, the temples and tombs are even more impressive in real life than in photos.
Well-organised tour with a knowledgeable guide, but it can be a bit hot in the middle of the day.
Perfect way to see Cairo in a few days, pyramids, bazaar, and old mosques all in one trip.
The tour was excellent, but Cairo traffic made some transfers a bit longer than expected.
Two incredible weeks of diving, kitesurfing, and relaxed evenings in Dahab.
So many activities to choose from, great if you like to stay active, but not much time for doing nothing.
Siwa is magical, quiet oasis, warm people, and the clearest night sky I’ve ever seen.
Loved the desert camp and salt lakes, but the journey from Cairo is quite long.
Exactly what we wanted, simple Bedouin camp, quiet desert, and unbelievable stargazing in Sinai.
Very peaceful experience and great guides; just be ready for cool temperatures at night.
Nice mix of lakes, waterfalls, and desert views, perfect short escape from Cairo.
Beautiful scenery and a relaxed pace, though the day at the waterfalls was a bit crowded.
Incredible rock formations. The 4x4 ride was thrilling, though very bumpy in certain sections.
A comprehensive tour of the city. The Egyptian Museum was fascinating, but our guide rushed us a bit at the end.
A surprisingly beautiful green spot in the desert. The waterfalls were refreshing and picturesque. Highly recommend!
Nice trip overall, but the weather was unusually cold by the lake. Definitely bring a light jacket if you go in winter.
Absolutely breathtaking oasis. The hot springs were incredibly relaxing after the long journey from the city.
Saw a turtle and several stingrays! The crew was fantastic and really prioritized our safety in the water.
The hike was much harder than advertised. Beautiful views, but make sure you are in good physical shape.
Camping under the stars here is a must-do. The Bedouin dinner prepared for us was easily the best meal of our trip.
The Valley of the Kings is magnificent. A bit crowded, but our guide navigated us perfectly to avoid the worst lines.
Laid-back vibes and excellent windsurfing. The town has great energy, and I wish I could have stayed another week!
Beautiful stargazing experience, but the group size was a bit too large for my taste. Still very memorable.
Waking up for the sunrise over the mountains made the brutal early morning start totally worth it. Truly awe-inspiring.
Stunning landscapes that look like surreal chalk sculptures. An unforgettable night away from civilization.
The Pyramids are breathtaking in person. Be prepared for aggressive vendors, though, which can get a little tiring.
A great break from the city noise. The pottery village nearby was a charming bonus we weren't expecting.
The falls aren't massive, but the surrounding scenery and sand dunes are very unique and photogenic.
Floating in the salt lakes is an experience I'll never forget. A magical place that feels entirely untouched.
Amazing to see the ancient artifacts, though the traffic getting between sites in the city was pretty awful.
A really rewarding trek. The path is clear, and our guide was very encouraging when the incline got steep.
Visibility in the water was outstanding. The reef colors are out of this world and the fish were everywhere.
Way too crowded at the summit for sunrise. It was loud and hard to even get a good photo.
Fantastic food tour in Islamic Cairo. The local cuisine is phenomenal, especially the fresh falafel!
It was very cloudy so we couldn't see many stars unfortunately, but the camp atmosphere was still nice.
The crew was a bit disorganized on the first day of the dive trip, but things smoothed out eventually.
A spiritually uplifting journey. The monastery at the bottom is an amazing historical site to end the hike with.
Karnak Temple is incredibly massive. Make sure you bring plenty of water and comfortable walking shoes.
Chill vibes, great seafood, and fantastic diving. What more could you want from a beach getaway?
Unbelievable views of the Milky Way. Our guide brewed some fantastic Bedouin tea while we looked at the constellations.
Very disappointed. Our accommodation was dirty and the local tour guide didn't show up on time.
Incredible marine life on every dive. We even swam with a pod of wild dolphins on our second day!
Very windy night, and sand got into everything. The surreal rock formations were cool to photograph, though.
A hot air balloon ride over the ancient temples at dawn was the absolute highlight of my trip to Egypt.
It felt truly isolated in the best way possible. A deeply peaceful experience away from modern distractions.
We started the hike too late and completely missed the sunrise at the top. Poor coordination by the operators.
Great experience overall. The traditional food prepared by the local guides over the open campfire was superb.
Walking through the narrow alleys of Khan el-Khalili was a sensory overload, but so much fun to explore!
Great atmosphere by the water, but the main promenade is getting a bit too commercialized for my liking.
The Temple of the Oracle and Cleopatra's Spring were fantastic to explore. Highly recommended for history lovers.
Fascinating to see fossilized whale bones right in the middle of the desert. Wadi El Rayan was very picturesque.
The corals are incredibly vibrant. The boat was spotless and the staff was extremely friendly and helpful.
Incredible vistas along the trail. The descent was quite tough on the knees, but definitely worth the effort.
Beautifully preserved tombs, but the aggressive tipping culture everywhere we went in the city was exhausting.
Amazing cultural experience. The locals are incredibly hospitable and the natural beauty feels wonderfully untouched.
Saw several shooting stars. The sheer tranquility of the desert night is something everyone should experience once.
The camp facilities were a bit too basic, and it was freezing cold once the sun went down. Bring lots of layers!
It was decent, but it felt very rushed. We simply didn't have enough time to explore the waterfalls properly.
Hatshepsut's Temple was breathtaking against the cliff face. A true masterpiece of ancient architecture.
A bit crowded on the trail leading up, but the camaraderie among the hikers from different countries was fun.
Beautiful beaches and a very relaxed pace. Just what I needed to escape the city grind for a few days.
